facade_colour: dark red-brown brick with blue and gold commercial signage

context.street_character: Major arterial | 4+ lanes | Streetcar (510) | 48m ROW | Large-scale commercial

assessment.signage: Minh Chau Jewellery & Watches (bilingual); Chaoshan Beef Hot Pot (Chinese characters); Pho Hung adjacent

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: dark red-brown brick with blue and gold commercial signage
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Brick well-maintained. Ground floor has security lattice grille. Prominent arched window on 2nd floor with multi-pane fan-like glazing pattern.
photo_observations.window_details: 2nd floor: 1 large arched window with multi-pane glazing and fan-like pattern in the arch. Upper floor: double-hung sash with dark frames.
photo_observations.door_details: Behind security lattice grille, not visible
photo_observations.cornice_details: Consistent with HCD building_features listing. Details not clearly visible at night.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Prominent arched window head on 2nd floor with fan-like multi-pane glazing pattern. Decorative brickwork and cornice per HCD records.
photo_observations.storefront_description: Ground floor with security lattice grille. Blue sign with gold Chinese characters 明珠珠寶鐘錶金行 / MINH CHAU JEWELLERY & WATCHES / 352 SPADINA AVE / TEL:416-979-3882
photo_observations.signage_observed: Minh Chau Jewellery & Watches / 明珠珠寶鐘錶金行 / blue sign with gold characters. Adjacent: Milland Realty blue sign.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Ground floor modified for commercial use with security grille. Upper storeys retain original brick and arched window.
photo_observations.notes: Nighttime photo. Notable arched 2nd-floor window with fan-pattern glazing is a distinctive heritage feature. Security grille obscures ground floor.

facade_description: 352 Spadina Ave presents a street-facing facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 6.1 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a flat. The facade reads primarily as brick, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade is organized as a storefront base with 1 upper-window bay and 1 additional bay above, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, cornice.
